resources.string:
Gaple is a traditional Indonesian domino game played with a set of 28 double-six dominoes.


Setup:
 • 2-4 players
 • Each player gets 7 dominoes
 • Remaining dominoes form the boneyard


Starting:
 • Player with highest double starts
 • If no doubles, player with highest value domino starts
 • First domino can be placed in any orientation


Playing:
 • Players take turns adding dominoes to either end
 • Domino values must match the end values
 • If unable to play, draw from boneyard
 • If boneyard is empty, pass turn


Winning:
 • First player to use all dominoes wins
 • If game is blocked, player with lowest hand value wins
 • Winner scores points equal to sum of all opponents' remaining dominoes

Opening Strategy:
 • Play highest double first if possible
 • Control the game by playing dominoes that limit opponents
 • Keep track of played dominoes


Mid-game Strategy:
 • Block opponents by playing dominoes they can't match
 • Keep a variety of pip values in your hand
 • Watch for patterns in opponents' plays


End-game Strategy:
 • Play high-value dominoes early
 • Keep low-value dominoes for the end
 • Count remaining dominoes to predict opponents' hands


Advanced Tips:
 • Remember which dominoes have been played
 • Force opponents to draw from boneyard
 • Use doubles strategically to change game flow
 • Calculate probability of opponents having specific dominoes

Basic Scoring:
 • Winner gets points equal to sum of all opponents' remaining dominoes
 • Each domino's value is the sum of its pips
 • Doubles count as their full value


Point Values:
 • 0-0 = 0 points
 • 1-0 = 1 point
 • 1-1 = 2 points
 • 2-0 = 2 points
 • And so on...


Special Scoring:
 • "Gaple" (winning with 0-0) = Double points
 • Blocked game: Player with lowest hand value wins
 • Tournament play: Usually first to 100 or 150 points


Penalty Points:
 • If you can't play and boneyard is empty, you get penalty points
 • Penalty equals your remaining hand value
Camera permission is required for domino recognition
